original_title: In Situ Single-Molecule Fluorescence Insights into Pd Nanocatalyzed Deallylation
title: Watching single Pd atoms do the deallylation, not the ensemble average
blurb: Bulk measurements on Pd nanoparticle catalysts average away exactly what you want to know, which particles and which facets are doing the actual chemistry. Single-molecule fluorescence microscopy tracks product formation directly on the nanoparticle surface, tying deallylation activity to facet structure instead of inferring it from bulk kinetics.

original_title: Preparation, characterization, and calibration of two fundamental reagents for the immunoradiometric (IRMA-125I) thyroid-stimulating hormone assay.
title: Calibrating the reagents behind the old TSH radioimmunoassay
blurb: Prep and calibration work on the two core reagents for the IRMA-125I thyroid-stimulating hormone assay, the unglamorous standardization that has to happen before anyone trusts a thyroid number that comes out of it.

original_title: Structure and mechanism of inhibition of lysine demethylase 2A (KDM2A) by compound 183c
title: How compound 183c shuts down KDM2A
blurb: Structural and mechanistic work showing how 183c binds and inhibits the histone demethylase KDM2A, the actual binding mode behind the inhibition rather than just a potency number.

original_title: De novo design of selective kinase modulators
title: Miniproteins that flip a kinase on or off by grabbing the kinase domain directly
blurb: De novo designed, genetically encoded miniproteins bind FAK's kinase domain itself rather than some allosteric side pocket. Of 96 binders built to lock in different FAK conformations, 33 actually changed kinase activity, and the four strongest included two low-nanomolar inhibitors alongside activators from the same design pool. Same design process, opposite effects, which is the interesting part.

original_title: Gopa Iyer, MD, highlights data on anti-Nectin-4 ADC for metastatic urothelial carcinoma - Urology Times
title: Iyer's conference rundown on the anti-Nectin-4 ADC in urothelial cancer
blurb: Gopa Iyer walks through data on an anti-Nectin-4 ADC in metastatic urothelial carcinoma, a conference highlights recap rather than a fresh trial readout.

original_title: Riding toward Selectivity: Optimization of Covalent 7-Azaindole-Based BMX Kinase Inhibitors
title: Tuning a covalent BMX inhibitor for selectivity, not just potency
blurb: Optimization of covalent 7-azaindole-based BMX kinase inhibitors, chasing selectivity within the kinase family, usually the harder half of covalent inhibitor design once you've already got something that reacts.

original_title: [177Lu]Lu-NeoB in Combination With Ribociclib and Fulvestrant in Participants With ER+, HER2- and GRPR+ Advanced Breast Cancer
title: Stacking a GRPR radioligand onto ribociclib and fulvestrant
blurb: A Phase 1, Novartis-run, no longer recruiting, combining the GRPR-targeted radioligand Lu-177-NeoB with ribociclib and fulvestrant in ER+/HER2-negative, GRPR-positive advanced breast cancer. Standard endocrine backbone plus a radioligand bolted on, worth watching for whether GRPR turns into a real target outside prostate cancer.

original_title: Radiolabeled Mesoporous Silica Nanosystem Enables Long-Term Cell Tracking In Vivo.
title: Radiolabeled silica built for long-haul cell tracking
blurb: A mesoporous silica nanoparticle carrying a radiolabel, meant to follow transplanted cells in a living animal over the long term rather than the few days a typical fluorescent tag lasts.

original_title: Hydroaminoalkylation and Zinc Cross-linking: A Pathway to Recyclable Polybutadiene Materials
title: Making polybutadiene recyclable by hanging zinc off it
blurb: Hydroaminoalkylation bolts amine groups onto 1,2-polybutadiene (PBD50, ~70% 1,2-vinyl), which raises the glass transition temperature and stiffens it against flow. Those amines then coordinate zinc centers to cross-link the polymer, a metal-coordination network instead of a permanent covalent one, which is presumably the point if you want it recyclable.

original_title: Prostate-specific membrane antigen based diagnostic and therapeutic integration for prostate cancer management.
title: One PSMA platform, both the scan and the treatment
blurb: A review on PSMA-based theranostics for prostate cancer, pairing the diagnostic imaging and the targeted therapy on the same molecular platform rather than running them as separate tracks.

original_title: Heterogeneous catalytic deuteration using D 2 O and advances in the application of deuterium labelling
title: D2O as the deuterium source, heterogeneous catalysts doing the swap
blurb: A review rounding up heterogeneous catalytic deuteration with D2O and where the labeled products end up: deuterium metabolic imaging, deuterated luminescent materials, quantitative mass spec.

original_title: Palladium-Catalyzed (4 + 3) Cycloadditions of Salicylimines and 1,4-Enynes: Access to Benzazepines
title: Pd cycloaddition turns salicylimines and enynes into benzazepines
blurb: A palladium-catalyzed (4+3) cycloaddition between salicylimines and 1,4-enynes that assembles the seven-membered benzazepine ring in one step.

original_title: Meso-electron-withdrawing pentamethine cyanines achieve large anti-Stokes blue-emitting triplet-triplet annihilation upconversion
title: Fixing the anti-Stokes-vs-yield tradeoff in blue upconversion
blurb: Metal-free triplet-triplet annihilation upconversion usually makes you choose between a big anti-Stokes shift and a decent quantum yield when pushing far-red light to blue emission. Parking electron-withdrawing groups at the meso position of a pentamethine cyanine narrows the singlet-triplet gap, suppresses the photoisomerization that eats efficiency, and drives intersystem crossing harder, which lifts the triplet energy above the plain meso-H version.

original_title: Valuable carbon nanomaterials directly prepared from CO 2 via sonication in pure water
title: Carbon nano-onions from limestone, no furnace involved
blurb: Sonicating CaCO3/Ca(OH)2 in plain water at room temperature pulls atmospheric CO2 into carbon-nano-onion-enriched material with 20-30 nm concentric shells. No pyrolysis, no exotic solvent, just ultrasound. Whether that holds up past a beaker is the part I'd want to see before calling it a CO2 capture route.

original_title: X-ray Crystallography-Guided Design and Synthesis of Cyclopentyl Heteroaryl Carboxylic Acid-Based Inhibitors of the SARS-CoV-2 Nsp3 Macrodomain (Mac1)
title: Crystal structures steer a new class of Mac1 inhibitors
blurb: Structure-guided medicinal chemistry builds cyclopentyl heteroaryl carboxylic acids against the SARS-CoV-2 Nsp3 macrodomain, Mac1.

original_title: Quantitative SPECT imaging for predicting therapeutic response to lutetium-177 DOTATATE in neuroendocrine tumors.
title: Can a SPECT scan predict if Lu-177 DOTATATE will work before you give it
blurb: Quantitative SPECT imaging tested as a predictor of response to Lu-177 DOTATATE therapy in neuroendocrine tumor patients, using the scan itself to forecast outcome rather than just tallying uptake after the fact.

original_title: Ligand immobilization levels for SPR
title: How much ligand is too much when all you want is steady-state affinity
blurb: Not a paper, a question: for SPR steady-state KD (no kinetics involved), is there an upper limit on ligand immobilization the way kinetics runs demand low density to dodge mass-transport artifacts? Looking for the rule of thumb the kinetics crowd already has.

original_title: All I measured in 2 years turned out to be background noise
title: Two years of data collapse into noise
blurb: New method, a long slog through the analysis pipeline, and the finish line is discovering every last data point is background. No signal, no paper, just two years accounted for.

original_title: Targeted antibody-drug conjugates for rhabdomyosarcoma and other FGFR4-expressing cancers.
title: An ADC aimed at FGFR4, rhabdomyosarcoma's calling card
blurb: Antibody-drug conjugates targeting FGFR4, the surface receptor that flags rhabdomyosarcoma along with a handful of other cancers that express it, as a way to route the payload straight to the tumor.

original_title: Actineer Achieves Sustainable Radium-226 Recycling to Advance Reliable Commercial-Scale Production of Actinium‑225 for Global Radiopharmaceutical Programs - Business Wire
title: Actineer says it's cracked the Ac-225 supply bottleneck by recycling radium
blurb: The pitch: recycle radium-226 instead of burning through fresh supply, and you get commercial-scale actinium-225 for radiopharma programs. It's a press release, so the numbers are the company's own until someone outside Actineer confirms them.

original_title: Neutrophil Elastase-Responsive Linkers for Precision Antibody–Drug Conjugates
title: A linker that only unclips when neutrophil elastase shows up
blurb: An ADC linker built to release its payload specifically in the presence of neutrophil elastase, aiming for tighter, more localized drug release than a generic cleavable linker.

original_title: Optimizing FAPI homodimers for theranostic applications: The impact of the linker design in DOTAGA.Glu2.(FAPI)2 and DOTAGA.PEG2.Glu.(FAPI)2
title: Two FAPI homodimer linkers, head to head for tumor retention
blurb: DOTAGA.Glu2.(FAPI)2 and DOTAGA.PEG2.Glu.(FAPI)2, both labeled with gallium-68 and lutetium-177 and run through the full workup: protein binding, internalization in FAP-positive cancer-associated fibroblasts, and biodistribution, metabolic stability and clearance in PC3 tumor-bearing mice. The point is finding which linker actually keeps the tracer parked on the tumor long enough to matter for imaging and therapy alike.
venue: European Journal of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging

original_title: Actineer Achieves Sustainable Radium-226 Recycling to Advance Reliable Commercial-Scale Production of Actinium‑225 for Global Radiopharmaceutical Programs - Yahoo Finance UK
title: Actineer's radium recycling play for actinium-225 supply
blurb: Recycled radium-226 as feedstock for scaling up actinium-225 production, pitched at the growing radiopharmaceutical pipeline. Still just a company announcement, so treat the "commercial-scale" and "reliable" claims as marketing copy until independent buyers confirm them.

original_title: Evaluation of 161 Tb-Labeled DOTA-Ibandronate for Targeted Radionuclide Therapy in Bone Metastases
title: Terbium-161 hitching a ride on a bone-seeking bisphosphonate
blurb: DOTA-ibandronate labeled with terbium-161, leaning on the bisphosphonate's affinity for bone mineral to steer a targeted radionuclide therapy straight into bone metastases.

original_title: Evaluation of (161)Tb-Labeled DOTA-Ibandronate for Targeted Radionuclide Therapy in Bone Metastases.
title: Tb-161 DOTA-ibandronate, aimed at bone mets
blurb: Terbium-161 attached to DOTA-ibandronate, using the bisphosphonate's pull toward bone mineral to get a targeted radionuclide therapy into bone metastases.

original_title: Predicting tumour and organ dose in α-radiopharmaceutical therapy: a 89Zr-based dosimetric approach for 225Ac
title: Can a Zr-89 scan predict what an Ac-225 dose will actually do?
blurb: Alpha emitters are a pain to image directly, so this tests whether zirconium-89-labeled antibodies can stand in for actinium-225-labeled ones when predicting tumor and organ dose. Two anti-CAIX antibodies, hG250 and an FcRn-binding-knockout version, were each labeled with both isotopes and compared head-to-head at matched protein dose in mice bearing kidney cancer tumors.
venue: European Journal of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging

original_title: A Cathepsin B -Cleavable Self-Immolative Linker for the Delivery of Hydroxyl-Containing Payloads in Antibody–Drug Conjugates
title: An ADC linker built for payloads without an amine to grab
blurb: A cathepsin B-cleavable, self-immolative linker made specifically to carry hydroxyl-bearing payloads, the kind that are normally awkward to attach since ADC linker chemistry usually wants an amine to hook onto.

original_title: Optimizing FAPI homodimers for theranostic applications: The impact of the linker design in DOTAGA.Glu(2).(FAPI)(2) and DOTAGA.PEG(2).Glu.(FAPI)(2).
title: Comparing FAPI homodimer linker chemistries
blurb: Two DOTAGA-based FAPI homodimers, differing only in the linker joining their two FAP-binding arms, built to see whether that linker design changes how well the tracer holds onto a tumor for theranostic use.
venue: Eur J Nucl Med Mol Imaging

original_title: Receptor-Targeted Folic Acid Ligand–Drug Conjugate of Exatecan Leads to Complete Tumor Regression in the Preclinical Tumor Model
title: A folate-targeted exatecan conjugate that clears tumors in mice
blurb: Exatecan riding a folic acid ligand straight to folate receptor-positive tumors, and in the preclinical model it wipes them out completely. Preclinical still means mice, not people, but a full regression result is the kind of number that gets a program funded.

original_title: Direct Amination of BODIPY via Cu(II)-Catalyzed C–H Activation: A One-Step Method for Drug/Chelator/Biomolecule Coupling
title: One step to stick anything onto a BODIPY dye
blurb: A Cu(II)-catalyzed C-H amination installs an amine handle on BODIPY directly, skipping the usual multistep dye functionalization. That handle is where you'd hang a drug, chelator, or biomolecule, so it's really a coupling shortcut dressed up as a dye paper.

original_title: Hybrid Macrocyclic Peptides — Synthetic Strategies to Diversify and Cyclize Peptide Libraries in Phage Display Selections
title: Making phage display's peptide hits actually druggable
blurb: Phage display is good at finding macrocyclic peptide binders but bad at handing you something stable or permeable, so this rounds up the synthetic tricks for bolting nonpeptidic scaffolds and cyclization chemistry onto those hits afterward. It's a strategy roundup rather than one new reaction, aimed at closing the gap between what selection finds and what medicinal chemistry needs to move it toward the clinic.

original_title: Cross-linking mass spectrometry: Workflow enhancements for mapping large-scale interactomes.
title: Better workflow tools for XL-MS interactome mapping
blurb: Workflow tweaks to cross-linking mass spec meant to make mapping large-scale interactomes more tractable, the usual bottleneck in this field being how far you can scale before it falls over.

original_title: Site-specific profiling of lysine itaconylation via a thiol-based probe
title: A thiol probe for finding itaconylated lysines
blurb: A thiol-based chemical probe that maps lysine itaconylation site by site, giving this modification an actual method to locate it on instead of inferring it from bulk data.

original_title: Glenmark Pharma Wins India's 1st Phase 3 Approval for Trastuzumab Rezetecan Cancer Trial - Sahi
title: Glenmark clears India's first phase 3 for trastuzumab rezetecan
blurb: Glenmark picked up India's first phase 3 approval to run trials of trastuzumab rezetecan, the HER2-targeted ADC, in cancer patients there. A regulatory milestone more than a data readout, worth watching once the trial itself reads out.

original_title: The Role of Contract Manufacturing in Antibody–Drug Conjugate (ADC) Drug Substance Development – Strategies for Successful Engagement
title: The CDMO playbook for actually getting your ADC made
blurb: A rundown on how sponsors should pick and manage contract manufacturers for ADC drug substance, given how many hand-offs a bioconjugate supply chain has. Industry strategy advice rather than new science, more useful to whoever's picking a CDMO than to the bench chemist.

original_title: Bioorthogonal labeling of sialic acid isomers for detection of glycoconjugates by mass spectrometry imaging and microscopy.
title: Telling sialic acid isomers apart with bioorthogonal tags
blurb: A protocol for bioorthogonally labeling sialic acid isomers so they can actually be distinguished in mass spec imaging and microscopy, rather than lumped together as one glycan signal.

original_title: A novel NAD(P)H:quinone oxidoreductase 1-responsive vascular endothelial growth factor-targeted antibody-drug conjugate induces dual apoptosis and ferroptosis in glioblastoma.
title: An ADC that kills glioblastoma two ways at once
blurb: A VEGF-targeted ADC built with an NQO1-responsive linker, so the payload releases specifically where that enzyme runs hot, and in glioblastoma it drives both apoptosis and ferroptosis rather than just one death pathway. Two mechanisms out of one conjugate is the interesting part, not just another VEGF ADC.

original_title: Injectable conductive chitosan/dextran/polypyrrole hydrogel for cardiac repair with potential application in radiation-induced cardiac injury.
title: A conductive hydrogel injected straight into damaged heart tissue
blurb: A chitosan/dextran/polypyrrole hydrogel meant to be injected into the heart, conductive enough to matter for cardiac repair, with an eye toward treating radiation-induced cardiac injury specifically. Still a materials paper looking for its clinical niche, but that niche is a real and underserved one.

original_title: Electron Activated Dissociation Technique for Middle-Down Mass Spectrometric Analysis of Disulphide Bonds and Free Thiols: Application in Characterising Monoclonal Antibody Biotherapeutic Fragments
title: Mapping mAb disulfide bonds and free thiols without digesting them first
blurb: A middle-down mass spec method that uses electron activated dissociation to read out disulfide bonds and free thiols straight off antibody fragments, instead of the usual labeled or label-free bottom-up digest workflows. The point is catching the scrambled or reduced disulfides that manufacturing can introduce, since those are the ones that quietly wreck stability and safety.

original_title: Cell-drug conjugates: a novel drug delivery system for cancer therapy
title: Loading whole cells as the delivery vehicle instead of an antibody
blurb: A review of cell-drug conjugates, cells used to ferry cytotoxic payloads into tumors, covering the case for them as a delivery platform and surveying the clinical trials already running. It's a survey piece, not new data, and ends with the standard list of hurdles left before wider translation.

original_title: Direct, Environmentally Friendly Recovery of Copper from E-Waste Using Picolinic Acid and Its Upcycling to Cu 2 O for Sustainable Propargylation of Isatins
title: Turning circuit-board copper into the catalyst for its own next reaction
blurb: Picolinic acid selectively strips copper out of waste circuit boards under optimized leaching conditions, and rather than stopping at 'recovered metal,' the copper gets converted to Cu2O and used to catalyze propargylation of isatins. E-waste remediation with a synthesis paper welded onto the back end.

original_title: Review: Phosphorus-33 - Analysis of nuclear reactor production methods for potential application in radionuclide therapy for bone metastases.
title: Could phosphorus-33 be the next bone-seeking therapy isotope
blurb: A review sizing up reactor production routes for P-33 as a candidate radionuclide for treating bone metastases. Just the production-methods case, no new therapy data attached.

original_title: A pneumolysin mutant (ΔA146 ply) induces pyroptosis in triple-negative and HER2-positive breast cancer cells via the Caspase-1/GSDME pathway: a potential antibody-drug conjugate payload.
title: A bacterial toxin mutant that makes breast cancer cells blow themselves up
blurb: A pneumolysin mutant, ΔA146 ply, triggers pyroptosis in triple-negative and HER2-positive breast cancer cells via the Caspase-1/GSDME pathway. The pitch is repurposing it as an antibody-drug conjugate payload, swapping the field's usual apoptosis-inducing toxins for one that ruptures the cell instead.

original_title: Discovery of a Thermostable Nigerose Phosphorylase for the Efficient Chemoenzymatic Radiosynthesis of a S. aureus –Targeted 18 F-Disaccharide
title: A heat-tolerant enzyme built to make an F-18 sugar tracer for staph
blurb: A newly discovered thermostable nigerose phosphorylase enables a chemoenzymatic route to an 18F-labeled disaccharide aimed at imaging S. aureus infections, letting the enzyme do the synthesis work that conventional radiochemistry would make painful.

original_title: Optimization of [18F]FBPA production for clinical translation through precursor development and manufacturing route evaluation
title: Getting [18F]FBPA production ready for routine clinical use
blurb: New precursor chemistry plus a manufacturing route evaluation for [18F]FBPA, the boron neutron capture therapy tracer, aimed at making production reliable enough for the clinic instead of a one-off synthesis each time.

original_title: Evaluation of treatment efficacy of the (161)Tb- and (177)Lu-Labeled GRPR antagonists AMTG and RM2 in a mouse model of prostate cancer.
title: Terbium-161 vs. lutetium-177 on the same GRPR antagonist scaffold
blurb: A mouse prostate cancer study comparing treatment efficacy of the GRPR antagonists AMTG and RM2 labeled with either Tb-161 or Lu-177, isotope swapped on otherwise identical targeting molecules. That isolates exactly what Tb-161's extra Auger electrons are supposed to buy over standard Lu-177.
venue: Eur J Nucl Med Mol Imaging

original_title: Asahi Kasei Life Science Acquires License for Novel Antibody-Drug Conjugate Technology from The Noguchi Institute – A Major Milestone for More Efficient and Safer Cancer Treatments - AOL.com
title: Asahi Kasei licenses an ADC platform from Japan's Noguchi Institute
blurb: A licensing deal, not a launch: Asahi Kasei Life Science picks up antibody-drug conjugate technology from the Noguchi Institute. The rest is standard press-release phrasing about 'more efficient and safer cancer treatments,' so save the excitement for actual data.

original_title: Oxidant-free Au(I)/Au(III) catalysis: Rational development of hemilabile proximity-guided N-heterocyclic carbenes
title: Gold catalysis that skips the oxidant by keeping Au(I) and Au(III) close together
blurb: Hemilabile N-heterocyclic carbene ligands are designed to hold gold's two oxidation states in proximity, letting Au(I)/Au(III) catalysis cycle without an external chemical oxidant. Redox handled by ligand geometry instead of by dumping in more reagent.

original_title: Relay energy transfer paradigm enabled the photocatalytic enantioselective dearomative [4+2] cycloadditions of quinolines with alkenes
title: Quinolines cyclize enantioselectively via relay energy transfer
blurb: A photocatalytic relay energy transfer setup drives dearomative [4+2] cycloadditions between quinolines and alkenes, and does it enantioselectively.

original_title: Peptide Thioester and Triazole Derivatives Through On‐Resin Dual‐Modification of Peptide Thiosulfonates
title: Two modifications, one peptide, one resin step
blurb: Most on-resin late-stage peptide modification hits one site and leaves the rest alone. This uses peptide thiosulfonates to install thioester and triazole handles at two different spots at once, widening how weird you can make a peptide before it ever comes off the resin.

original_title: Enantioconvergent alkylation of boron-stabilized organomagnesiums with unactivated alkyl halides using nickel catalysis
title: Racemic Grignards go enantioconvergent with a boron assist
blurb: Racemic alkylmagnesium reagents usually can't be pushed to a single enantiomer in C(sp3)-C(sp3) cross-coupling because side reactions take over. Stabilizing them with boron and coupling with unactivated alkyl iodides under nickel catalysis gets an enantioconvergent reaction anyway, a fix aimed squarely at the side-reaction problem that's dogged this class of coupling.

original_title: Diastereoselective Pyrrolidine Formation via Pd(II)-Catalyzed Cyclization of γ -C(sp 3 )–H Bond of Substituted Amino Acids with Morita–Baylis–Hillman Acetate
title: Pyrrolidines built off a C-H bond nobody usually touches
blurb: Pd(II) activates the gamma C(sp3)-H bond on substituted amino acids and cyclizes it with Morita-Baylis-Hillman acetate, delivering pyrrolidines diastereoselectively.

original_title: (99)ᵐTc-DTPA-OM: Cardiac-myosin targeted SPECT agent for non-invasive myocardial infarction imaging.
title: A SPECT tracer aimed at exposed cardiac myosin
blurb: 99mTc-DTPA-OM binds cardiac myosin, which is normally tucked away and only becomes accessible once heart tissue dies, giving a non-invasive SPECT route to spot myocardial infarction.

original_title: Preferential site occupation by sulfur ligands regulates hydrogen activation on Pd catalysts for selective hydrogenation
title: Why sulfur-dosed Pd gets picky, not just poisoned
blurb: Sulfide-modified Pd catalysts favor nitro hydrogenation over dechlorination, and the mechanism behind that had been murky. Combining CO chemisorption data with DFT, this shows sulfide ligands preferentially camp out on Pd's most active surface sites first, meaning the selectivity comes from which sites get blocked, not just how many.

original_title: Two-isotope imaging could guide targeted alpha therapy for metastatic prostate cancer - Medical Xpress
title: Two isotopes, one scan, for steering prostate alpha therapy
blurb: Pairing two isotopes in a single imaging approach to help guide targeted alpha therapy toward metastatic prostate cancer.

original_title: Oxetane-to-azetidine skeletal editing
title: Swapping an oxetane's oxygen for azetidine's nitrogen directly
blurb: A non-redox skeletal editing strategy converts oxetanes straight into azetidines instead of building the azetidine ring from scratch, opening rapid access to structurally diverse azetidine analogues for drug discovery.

original_title: Glutathione-Triggered Unleashing of Protease K Prodrug Suppressed Primary Tumors and Its Metastasis with Stimulated Anti-tumor Immunity.
title: A proteinase K prodrug that wakes up inside tumors
blurb: Proteinase K gets caged until glutathione, the reducing agent tumors are swimming in, springs it loose. Once active it suppressed primary tumors and their metastases while also priming antitumor immunity.

original_title: Improving Pharmacokinetics of PSMA/GRPR-Targeting Radiolabeled Heterodimer through Enhanced Albumin Binding
title: Giving a PSMA/GRPR tracer something to hang onto
blurb: This radiolabeled PSMA/GRPR-targeting heterodimer gets a pharmacokinetics boost from enhanced albumin binding, aimed at keeping it in circulation longer.

original_title: Radiolabeling, characterization, and bioevaluation of <sup>99m</sup>Tc-Itaconic acid for detection of inflammation in mice
title: A Tc-99m tag for itaconic acid, aimed at spotting inflammation
blurb: 99mTc-itaconic acid, radiolabeled and characterized, then tested in mice as a candidate tracer for imaging inflammation.

original_title: Amide-Directed Palladium-Catalyzed Direct Cage C–H Sulfenylation of o -Carboranes
title: Getting palladium onto the carborane cage itself
blurb: An amide directing group steers a palladium catalyst onto the cage C-H bonds of o-carboranes for sulfenylation, the kind of directed C-H chemistry usually reserved for arenes, now done on a boron cluster instead.

original_title: Synthesis of Z‑allylamines via masked alkenyl anions from alkynyl tetracoordinate boron compounds
title: A metal-free route to Z-allylamines, dr past 20:1
blurb: A three-component cascade, alkynyl tetracoordinate boron plus amine plus aldehyde, runs through a masked alkenyl anion and comes out exclusively Z with no metal catalyst needed. Chiral amines push the diastereomeric ratio past 20:1, and the substrate scope is broad.

original_title: Programmable arene ring opening unlocks the diversification of phenols
title: Cracking the phenol ring open to make room for nitrogen
blurb: Phenols are cheap and everywhere but their aromatic ring has resisted selective remodeling. A programmable nitrogenative ring-opening method breaks that ring and installs nitrogen, turning phenols into N-functionalized scaffolds useful for both synthesis and polymer materials.

original_title: A Glimpse into the Initial Microsecond of Biomolecular Condensation
title: Catching a biomolecular condensate in its first microsecond
blurb: A look at what's happening right as a biomolecular condensate first forms, on a microsecond timescale that's normally too fast to see.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Circular Bivalent Aptamer Chimeras Leveraging LDLR-Mediated Lysosomal Shuttling for Targeted Protein Degradation
title: Aptamers that hijack LDLR to drag proteins to the lysosome
blurb: A circular bivalent aptamer grabs a target protein on one end and LDLR on the other, then rides the receptor's own lysosomal shuttling to degrade it. Targeted protein degradation without the PROTAC playbook's E3 ligase machinery.

original_title: Controlling Product Selectivity by Modulating H-Bonding and the Rate of Hydrogen Elimination and Hydrogen Transfer from an Ir(III)-H Complex
title: Tuning an Ir-H complex's hydrogen bonds to pick the product
blurb: Dialing in the hydrogen bonding around an Ir(III)-hydride shifts the balance between hydrogen elimination and hydrogen transfer, letting you steer which product comes out. A selectivity knob built from noncovalent interactions rather than a new ligand.

original_title: Visible-Light-Driven Enantioselective Free Radical Addition to Aldimines via Direct C–H Functionalization
title: Visible light turns a C-H bond into a chiral radical addition
blurb: Direct C-H functionalization generates a radical under visible light that adds to aldimines enantioselectively, no prefunctionalized radical precursor required.

original_title: A printable blue-light photocurable gelatin methacryloyl/silk fibroin hydrogel loaded with salvianolic acid B for enhanced spinal cord repair.
title: A printable, drug-loaded hydrogel aimed at spinal cord repair
blurb: Gelatin methacryloyl and silk fibroin, cured with blue light and loaded with salvianolic acid B, printed into a scaffold built for spinal cord repair.

original_title: Visible-Light Photocatalytic Multicomponent Ritter-Type Reactions with Aryl and Alkyl Bromides as Carbon Radical Precursors
title: Aryl and alkyl bromides feed a photocatalytic Ritter reaction
blurb: Visible-light photocatalysis turns aryl and alkyl bromides into carbon radicals that feed a multicomponent Ritter-type reaction, a radical route into a reaction that's normally run under harsh acid.

original_title: Iridium‐Catalyzed Ionic Hydrogenation of Multi‐Aza Heterocycles
title: Ionic hydrogenation finally tackles multi-nitrogen heterocycles
blurb: An iridium-catalyzed ionic hydrogenation route for saturating heterocycles stacked with multiple nitrogens, the kind of sp3-rich scaffolds that beat their flat aromatic cousins in the clinic but have been a pain to access. The paper's whole premise is that hydrogenation tech for these polyaza rings has lagged behind, so this is filling that gap rather than debuting a flashy new molecule.

original_title: Pd-Catalyzed Dehydrogenative C–H Alkynylation/Annulation/Hydration Cascade of Benzylamines with Bromoalkyne: Stereoselective Synthesis of ( E )-3-Methyleneisoindolinones
title: Pd cascade turns benzylamines into methyleneisoindolinones in one pot
blurb: A palladium-catalyzed cascade stringing together C–H alkynylation, annulation, and hydration on benzylamines and a bromoalkyne partner, landing stereoselectively on (E)-3-methyleneisoindolinones. Three bond-forming steps, one pot, defined geometry.

original_title: Enantioselective Construction of Bridged Biaryl Atropisomers With Medium‐ and Macrocyclic Architectures
title: A review of how to bend biaryls into rings and keep their chirality
blurb: A review of catalytic strategies for building bridged biaryl atropisomers into medium and macrocyclic rings, locking axial chirality into frameworks that are compact but conformationally tangled. Ground covered rather than broken, but useful if you're picking a macrocyclization strategy that won't also scramble your stereocenter.

original_title: Determinants of Stereoselectivity in Monoterpene Synthases
title: What actually sets the stereochemistry in monoterpene synthases
blurb: A look at the enzymatic determinants behind why monoterpene synthases commit to one stereochemical outcome over another. Thin on specifics here, but the question is the useful part, since these enzymes are notorious for turning one substrate into a dozen different terpene skeletons.

original_title: Machine Learning-Assisted Development of High-Performance Ethanol Synthesis Catalysts via CO 2 Hydrogenation
title: Letting ML pick the catalyst for turning CO2 into ethanol
blurb: Machine learning guiding development of catalysts for CO2 hydrogenation straight to ethanol, instead of the usual trial-and-error screening. No performance numbers to go on yet, but the pitch is a shortcut to better catalysts for a reaction everyone wants to work.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Divergent Synthesis of Bicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes and Bicyclo[3.1.1]heptenes via Controllable Tandem Annulation/Rearrangement of Bicyclo[1.1.0]butanes and Vinyl Diazo Compounds
title: One strained precursor, two different bicyclic cores depending on how you push it
blurb: A tandem annulation/rearrangement of bicyclo[1.1.0]butanes with vinyl diazo compounds that branches to either bicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes or bicyclo[3.1.1]heptenes depending on conditions, from the same starting materials. These strained bicyclics are the current benzene-replacement du jour in med chem, so a divergent route to two of them off one feedstock is the useful part.

original_title: Emerging enzymatic strategies for the synthesis of α-tertiary amino acids
title: Getting enzymes to make amino acids with a quaternary twist
blurb: A survey of enzymatic approaches to α-tertiary amino acids, the ones with a fully substituted alpha carbon that make them notoriously hard to make asymmetrically by any other route. A review rather than a new method, so treat it as a map of what's out there.

original_title: Axially chiral thiophene scaffolds: configurational stability and circularly polarized luminescence
title: Thiophene scaffolds that hold their twist and glow circularly
blurb: Aryl-substituted bidithienothiophenes (BDTT-Ar) built with high enough inversion barriers to stay locked in one axial configuration, resolved into single enantiomers that show circularly polarized luminescence. Configurationally stable and CPL-active in the same scaffold is the combination that makes this worth a look for chiral optoelectronics rather than just another CPL curiosity.

original_title: Second-Line Sacituzumab Tirumotecan Plus Anlotinib for Advanced Driver Gene-Negative Non-Squamous NSCLC
title: Trop2 ADC plus a TKI for NSCLC patients with no driver mutation to target
blurb: A phase 2 trial pairing sacituzumab tirumotecan, a Trop2-directed antibody-drug conjugate, with the multi-kinase inhibitor anlotinib as second-line treatment for advanced non-squamous NSCLC in patients lacking a targetable driver mutation. Still active, not recruiting, no readout yet, so this is one to bookmark rather than react to.

original_title: Covalent remodeling of CRBN creates a non-canonical neosubstrate interface with NTAQ1
title: A covalent CRBN glue that reaches a neosubstrate the reversible drugs can't touch
blurb: The molecular glue EM12-FS covalently modifies CRBN's His353, and the cryo-EM structure shows why that matters: imidazylation repositions the histidine just enough to clear a steric clash that normally keeps NTAQ1 out of reach for reversible IMiDs, opening a non-canonical interface held together by a T-shaped CH/pi contact with NTAQ1's Phe126. A structural explanation for how covalent CRBN engagement can recruit neosubstrates the whole reversible-glue toolkit simply can't reach.

original_title: Phased affinity-controlled delivery of vascular endothelial growth factor, fibroblast growth factor-2, and platelet derived growth factor enhances in vitro angiogenesis.
title: Timing the growth-factor cocktail instead of dumping it all at once
blurb: Affinity binding lets VEGF, FGF-2, and PDGF release in a controlled sequence rather than all together, and that phased delivery beats a single-dose mix at driving angiogenesis in vitro. Still a dish, not a wound, but the staggered-release idea is the point.

original_title: Avidin-Functionalized Gold Nanoparticles Enable Cationic Isotachophoresis for Enhanced Lateral Flow Assays
title: Squeezing more sensitivity out of the lateral flow strip
blurb: Avidin-coated gold nanoparticles get stacked and concentrated via cationic isotachophoresis before they hit the test line, boosting signal on an otherwise ordinary lateral flow assay. Same cheap strip format, just with an electrophoretic preconcentration step bolted on front.

original_title: Nickel-Catalyzed Deoxyvinylation of Benzyl Alcohols via Cross-Electrophile Coupling with Vinyl Bromides
title: Turning a benzylic alcohol straight into a vinyl group
blurb: Nickel-catalyzed cross-electrophile coupling swaps the OH on a benzyl alcohol directly for a vinyl group pulled from a vinyl bromide. One less activation step than the usual route to that bond.

original_title: Visible-Light-Promoted Intermolecular Dearomative Hydrocyanoalkylation of Indoles with Oxime Esters Toward Functionalized Indolines
title: Breaking indole aromaticity to build cyanoalkylated indolines
blurb: Visible light drives an intermolecular dearomative coupling of indoles with oxime esters, installing a cyanoalkyl group while flattening the indole into an indoline. Photocatalysis doing dearomatization that usually needs harsher conditions.

original_title: Isosteric Engineering of Enzymes: Overcoming Activity–Stability Trade‐Offs by Site‐Selective CH → N Substitutions
title: Swapping a CH for an N breaks the enzyme's activity-vs-stability ceiling
blurb: PET hydrolases pushed through exhaustive mutagenesis hit a wall where more activity costs thermal stability, and the usual fix, noncanonical amino acids, is too expensive and structurally disruptive for industrial use. Site-selective CH→N substitutions, a subtler isosteric swap using only canonical chemistry, got PETases past that ceiling instead.

original_title: Separable syn/anti Bis-Boron Dyes with Stereochemistry-Dependent Packing and Aggregated-State Emission
title: Same bis-boron dye, two stereoisomers, two different glow patterns
blurb: Syn and anti isomers of a bis-boron dye separate cleanly, and stereochemistry alone decides how the molecules pack and whether they light up in the aggregated state. Packing, not electronics, turning out to be the dial for aggregation-induced emission.

original_title: Prospective Pilot Study of 11C-acetate and 18F-FDG with PET/CT and PET/MRI for Lesion Detection in Multiple Myeloma
title: Two tracers, two scanners, one hunt for myeloma lesions
blurb: A pilot study runs 11C-acetate and 18F-FDG through both PET/CT and PET/MRI head to head to see which combination catches more multiple myeloma lesions. It's a pilot, so read it as a first look rather than a verdict on which pairing wins.

original_title: Electrochemical Bromo-Alkyloxylation of Conjugated Alkenes: A Method to Access Bromo-Alkyloxy-Alkenes/Alkynes
title: Electrochemistry adds bromine and an alkoxy group across an alkene in one step
blurb: An electrochemical method difunctionalizes conjugated alkenes with a bromide and an alkyloxy group simultaneously, reaching bromo-alkyloxy-alkenes and alkynes without stoichiometric oxidants. Decent atom economy, if the scope goes beyond the usual model substrates.

original_title: Streptavidin Functionalized Hyaluronic Acid Hydrogels for Controlled and Customizable Drug Delivery.
title: A hyaluronic acid hydrogel you can swap payloads into
blurb: Grafting streptavidin onto hyaluronic acid hydrogels means any biotinylated drug just clips in, turning the release platform into something reusable across drugs instead of a one-shot gel. Biotin-streptavidin doing double duty as anchor and release dial.

original_title: Radioligand therapy for HER2-positive cancers enters human trials for the first time - Medical Xpress
title: HER2 radioligand therapy finally makes it into people
blurb: A radioligand therapy for HER2-positive cancers has entered its first human trial. First-in-human means they're checking safety and dosing, not whether it works yet.

original_title: Ultrafast Access to Indole-Fused Isocoumarin with Tunable Emission via Cascade Dual C–H/C–H Cross-Coupling
title: A cascade C–H coupling that builds its own fluorophore
blurb: A dual C–H/C–H cross-coupling cascade snaps together the indole-fused isocoumarin core in one pot, and the emission color shifts with substitution. Fast, and the tunability suggests it's aimed at fluorophore design, not just a scaffold flex.

original_title: Treatment of Human Glioblastoma Multiforme in NRG Mice by Convection-Enhanced Delivery of EGFR-Targeted or Nontargeted Auger Electron–Emitting 197g Hg-Labeled Gold Nanoparticles
title: Auger-emitting gold nanoparticles, dropped straight into a glioblastoma
blurb: Convection-enhanced delivery puts 197g-Hg-labeled gold nanoparticles, EGFR-targeted or plain, directly into glioblastoma tumors in NRG mice, letting the short-range Auger electrons do their damage locally. Injecting straight into the tumor bed sidesteps the blood-brain barrier problem that usually kills this class of approach; running both targeted and untargeted arms suggests they weren't sure targeting would even matter.

original_title: Enzyme-Activatable Fluorogenic Probes: Design Strategies, Biomedical Applications, and Future Perspectives
title: A map of fluorogenic probes that switch on where the enzyme is
blurb: A review of enzyme-activatable fluorogenic probes: how they're designed and where they've already been put to work in biomedicine, plus where the authors think the field goes next. Useful as an overview of the space rather than a new result of its own.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Annulation of aliphatic nitro compounds with β-styryl sulfonium salts toward the synthesis of isoxazoline N -oxides
title: A route to isoxazoline N-oxides with the substitution pattern nobody could reach
blurb: Annulating aliphatic nitro compounds with β-styryl sulfonium salts builds isoxazoline N-oxides carrying a substitution pattern that's normally a pain to install directly. The payoff is entirely in reaching that pattern, not in the reaction being flashy.

original_title: A comprehensive review of (11)C-, (18)F-, and (123/124/125)I-labeled radiotracers targeting cyclooxygenase-2 over the past two decades.
title: Twenty years of trying to image COX-2 with C-11, F-18, and iodine
blurb: A review tallying two decades of (11)C-, (18)F-, and (123/124/125)I-labeled tracers built to target COX-2. A roundup of what's been tried across the field rather than a new tracer itself.

original_title: Modulating Viscoelasticity and Live Cell Response with Fibrillar Peptide Polymers
title: Peptide polymers that let you dial in how gooey the gel feels to a cell
blurb: Fibrillar peptide polymers where viscoelasticity can be tuned separately from stiffness, and live cells respond differently depending on which one you turn. Useful because those two variables are usually locked together in matrix studies.

original_title: Base-Catalyzed Diversity-Orientated Synthesis of Tetra-/Dihydrothiophenecarboxamides and Preliminary Evaluation as Antifungal Agents
title: A base-catalyzed route to thiophenecarboxamides, tried out as antifungals
blurb: A base-catalyzed diversity-oriented synthesis reaches a set of tetra- and dihydrothiophenecarboxamides, which then get a first pass as antifungal agents. Early-stage screening, so the synthesis route is the real payoff here, not proven antifungal activity.

original_title: Carbene-Catalyzed Desymmetric Amidation Enables Modular Access to Planar Chiral Thioureas and Imides
title: Carbene catalysis breaks the symmetry to make planar chiral thioureas
blurb: A carbene-catalyzed desymmetric amidation opens a modular route to planar chiral thioureas and imides, turning a symmetric precursor straight into a single chiral product instead of resolving a mixture afterward.

original_title: Engineering the bacterial cellulose-forming surface as a programmable protein recruitment interface
title: Turning bacterial cellulose into a protein-display surface as it grows
blurb: Komagataeibacter intermedius normally just spins out cellulose at its cell surface; engineering that surface via Lpp'OmpA turns it into a display platform that recruits chosen proteins right as the cellulose forms, so functionalization happens for free during growth. First proof is a fluorescent protein stuck to the surface, so it's a demonstration more than a finished product.

original_title: Comparative intra-articular retention of [(177)Lu]Lu-MAA and (90)Y resin in a rat knee model.
title: Lu-177-MAA vs. Y-90 resin: which one actually stays put in the knee
blurb: A rat knee model compares how well [177Lu]Lu-MAA and (90)Y resin stick around intra-articularly versus leaking out. A retention comparison aimed at picking the better radiosynovectomy agent, not a therapeutic result on its own.

original_title: Application of electron diffraction to rapid structural elucidation of crude reaction products
title: Structure ID without ever purifying the reaction
blurb: Electron diffraction run straight on crude reaction mixtures, skipping the usual recrystallize-and-pray step just to find out what you made. Handy if it holds up outside the cherry-picked cases.

original_title: Mechanochemical Electron Donor–Acceptor (EDA)-Complex-Mediated Thioetherification Reaction via Site-Selective C–H Functionalization Using Aryl (DMIX)iodonium Salt
title: A ball mill and an EDA complex do the C–H thioetherification
blurb: Mechanochemistry plus an electron donor-acceptor complex activates a hypervalent aryl iodonium salt for site-selective C–H thioetherification, no photocatalyst, no solvent. Grinding is doing the job light usually does here.
